content.problemStatement:
Django follows the 
Model-View-Template (MVT)
 
pattern, a design architecture that separates concerns in web applications, making development more organized and scalable. 

----


- **Model (M)** – Handles the database and defines the structure of the data. 

- **View (V)** – Processes user requests, interacts with the Model, and returns the appropriate response. 

- **Template (T)** – Defines how the data is presented to the user using HTML and dynamic content. 

This separation allows for better code maintenance, reusability, and flexibility. 

The MVT pattern is similar to the **MVC (Model-View-Controller)** pattern used in other frameworks but is specifically tailored to Django.
